ex.en: The painter finished the portrait in a week.
ex.ko: 그 화가는 일주일 만에 초상화를 완성했다.

painter.en: a worker whose job is to cover walls or objects with paint

ex.en: We hired a painter to repaint the fence.
ex.ko: 우리는 울타리를 다시 칠하려고 페인트공을 고용했다.

ex.en: We've been pals since childhood.

pan.en: a wide metal container used for cooking food

ex.en: Heat some oil in the pan.

panic.en: suddenly feel so frightened that you cannot think clearly

ex.en: Don't panic, everything will be fine.

panic.en: a sudden overwhelming feeling of fear

ex.en: There was panic in the crowd when the alarm went off.
ex.ko: 경보가 울리자 군중 사이에 공황이 일었다.

part.en: leave someone or stop being together

ex.en: The two friends parted at the station.

part.en: a piece or section of something that combines with other pieces to make a whole

ex.en: This is the best part of the movie.
ex.ko: 이게 그 영화에서 가장 좋은 부분이야.

part.en: a piece that is used to make a machine or object

ex.en: We need to order some car parts.
ex.ko: 우리는 자동차 부품을 좀 주문해야 한다.

part.en: the character that an actor plays in a film or play

ex.en: She played the part of the queen.

partly.en: to some degree, but not completely

ex.en: The delay was partly due to bad weather.
ex.ko: 그 지연은 부분적으로 나쁜 날씨 때문이었다.

pass.en: move past or through a place

ex.en: We passed a small village on the way.
ex.ko: 우리는 가는 길에 작은 마을을 지나쳤다.

pass.en: give something to someone by putting it in their hand

ex.en: Could you pass the salt, please?

pass.en: succeed in a test or examination

ex.en: She passed her driving test on the first try.
ex.ko: 그녀는 첫 시도에 운전면허 시험에 합격했다.

pass.en: a card or document that allows you to enter a place or use a service

ex.en: You need a pass to enter the stadium.
Two years passed quickly.

passage.en: a short section of a piece of writing

ex.en: Read the passage and answer the questions.

passage.en: a long narrow area that connects one place to another

ex.en: They walked down a narrow passage to the exit.
ex.ko: 그들은 좁은 통로를 걸어 출구로 향했다.

passenger.en: a person traveling in a vehicle who is not driving or operating it

ex.en: The bus can carry fifty passengers.
ex.ko: 그 버스는 승객 50명을 태울 수 있다.

pasta.en: an Italian food made from flour and water, often served with sauce

ex.en: We had pasta for dinner.
We followed the path through the forest.

path.en: a course of action that leads towards a particular result

ex.en: She chose a difficult path to success.
ex.ko: 그녀는 성공으로 가는 어려운 길을 택했다.

patient.en: able to wait or accept problems without becoming annoyed

ex.en: Please be patient; the bus will come soon.
ex.ko: 조금만 참으세요, 버스는 곧 올 거예요.

patient.en: a person who is receiving medical treatment

ex.en: The doctor examined the patient carefully.

PC.en: a personal computer, especially one used at home or in an office

ex.en: He bought a new PC for gaming.

pea.en: a small round green vegetable that grows in pods

ex.en: She added peas to the soup.

peaceful.en: calm and free from disturbance or war

ex.en: It was a peaceful morning in the village.

peaceful.en: not using violence, especially in a protest

ex.en: They organized a peaceful protest.

pear.en: a sweet juicy fruit that is narrow at the top and round at the bottom

ex.en: She sliced a ripe pear for the salad.
ex.ko: 그녀는 샐러드를 위해 잘 익은 배를 썰었다.

pence.en: the plural of penny, a small unit of British money

ex.en: The chocolate bar costs eighty pence.

penfriend.en: someone you become friends with by writing letters, usually without meeting

ex.en: I have a penfriend in France.

pepper.en: a spicy powder used to flavor food, made from dried berries

ex.en: Add some salt and pepper to the soup.

pepper.en: a hollow vegetable, often red, green, or yellow, that can be mild or spicy

ex.en: She chopped a red pepper for the salad.

perfect.en: make something completely correct or as good as it can be

ex.en: She practiced for years to perfect her technique.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기술을 완벽하게 다듬기 위해 수년간 연습했다.

perfect.en: having no faults or flaws; as good as possible

ex.en: It was a perfect day for a picnic.

perfectly.en: in a way that has no faults; completely

ex.en: She sang the song perfectly.

perform.en: entertain an audience by singing, acting, or playing music

ex.en: The band performed at the festival.

perform.en: carry out a task or action

ex.en: The surgeon performed the operation successfully.

performance.en: a show performed in front of an audience

ex.en: We watched an amazing dance performance.

performance.en: how well someone or something does a task

ex.en: The team's performance improved this season.
ex.ko: 그 팀의 성과는 이번 시즌에 향상되었다.

perfume.en: a liquid with a pleasant smell that people put on their skin

ex.en: She wore a light floral perfume.

perhaps.en: used to say that something is possible but not certain

ex.en: Perhaps we should leave earlier.
ex.ko: 아마도 우리는 더 일찍 출발해야 할 것 같다.

permission.en: official agreement that lets you do something

ex.en: You need permission to park here.

personality.en: the set of qualities that make a person different from others

ex.en: She has a warm and friendly personality.
ex.ko: 그녀는 따뜻하고 친근한 성격을 가지고 있다.

personality.en: a person who is well known, especially in entertainment or media

ex.en: He became a famous TV personality.

petrol.en: fuel used in cars and other vehicles (British English)

ex.en: We stopped to buy some petrol.

petrol station.en: a place where you can buy petrol for your car

ex.en: There's a petrol station just around the corner.

photograph.en: take a picture of someone or something using a camera

ex.en: He photographed the sunset.

photograph.en: a picture made using a camera

ex.en: She showed us photographs from her trip.
ex.ko: 그녀는 여행에서 찍은 사진들을 우리에게 보여주었다.
